threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look confused at bus stops, waiting for the bus to actually show up. Locals know it's just a suggestion, mate.
Caught a whiff of jerk chicken on the 333 last night; turns out the driver stopped mid-route for a takeaway. Classic Brixton!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a group of kids using the tram tracks as a makeshift skateboard park, while the BVG was on strike, I knew this was home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a self-service beer tap at a Späti. Grab a cup, pour your own, and sip while chatting with locals. No fuss!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s a local when they call it 'the 24' instead of the ‘24 bus’ and can list all the pizza spots without googling.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy reading Shakespeare on the 49 this morning. Perfectly calm while the rest of us stewed in traffic. Only in SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think the fountains in d'Aritja Park are for show, but locals know they’re the best spot for chatting on a hot summer night.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their confounded look when faced with Gràcia's winding streets—like they've just exited a labyrinth.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think it’s a good idea to take the tube to London Fields instead of just walking through Victoria Park. Classic rookie mistake.
Caught a night bus home from Brixton last week; a guy serenaded the whole back row with '80s power ballads. Best part of the trip.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw my neighbor arguing passionately with a pigeon over a bagel on the corner of Flughafenstraße, I knew I was home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a spontaneous rooftop cinema screening among the graffiti, where even the city's hustle pauses for a random movie night.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they have a favorite taco spot that isn’t on Yelp. The best ones are hidden behind a laundromat or in a garage.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni bus today where the driver serenaded us with opera. Only in SF do you get a free concert while commuting.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all trendy bars, but miss the quiet corners where the real locals debate everything from fútbol to politics.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by the way they struggle with the metro ticket machines; most locals know to avoid the 'help' button.
